name: MaxCut (Annealing)
author: openqc-team
description: Solve MaxCut graph partitioning via quantum/simulated annealing. Same problem as qaoa-maxcut but uses annealing instead of gate-based QAOA. Compare results across paradigms: gate-based (QAOA) vs annealing on the same graph. QUBO formulation: Q_{ij} = -w for cut edges.

metadata.complexity_quantum: Annealing heuristic, no proven speedup for general graphs
metadata.academic_intro: MaxCut asks for a two-way split of a graph's nodes that cuts as many edges as possible. This template encodes it as a QUBO (energy = minus the cut size) and lets simulated annealing settle into a low-energy state.
metadata.expected_takeaway: Compare with qaoa-maxcut on the identical 4-node ring: gate-based QAOA and annealing attack the same problem through different computation models.

edges.description: Graph edges as [i, j] pairs. Default: the 4-node ring [[0,1],[1,2],[2,3],[3,0]] — the same graph as qaoa-maxcut.
